records.title: Soft acid Bi³⁺ doping in Li₂ZrCl₆ to enhance ionic conductivity and electrochemical stability

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li₂.₁₅Zr₀.₈₅Bi₀.₁₅Cl₆ was prepared using high-energy ball milling with LiCl, ZrCl₄, and BiCl₃ weighed in molar ratio of 2.15:0.85:0.15, ground and mixed in an agate mortar, then sealed in a ZrO₂ jar containing ZrO₂ balls and grinding was conducted in a ball mill at 1000 rpm for 15 h

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at room temperature using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy with stainless steel blocking electrodes on pellets pressed at 380 MPa
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: the ionic conductivity of modified Li₂.₁₅Zr₀.₈₅Bi₀.₁₅Cl₆ at room temperature is 4.9×10⁻⁴ S cm⁻¹

electrochemical_window.measurement_conditions: determined by cyclic voltammetry using Li|Li₆PS₅Cl-SE|SE+C cell structure with scan rate of 1 mV/s and voltage scan range of -0.5 V to 5 V
electrochemical_window.text_evidence: the reduction potential of LZBC is 2.19 V versus Li⁺/Li

records.title: A low-cost, lithium-rich zirconium-based oxyhalide solid electrolyte featuring an efficient ion transport structure

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li2ZrCl6 was synthesized using high-energy ball milling method from raw materials LiCl and ZrCl4

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at room temperature using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: the prototype material Li2ZrCl6 exhibiting an ionic conductivity of only 0.15 mS cm⁻¹

electrochemical_window.measurement_conditions: determined by cyclic voltammetry using Li|LPSCl|SE|SE + C battery configuration
electrochemical_window.text_evidence: LZC displayed oxidation peaks at 4.67 V vs Li⁺/Li and reduction potentials of 2.66 V relative to Li⁺/Li

records.title: A cost-effective and humidity-tolerant chloride solid electrolyte for lithium batteries

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li₂ZrCl₆ was mechanochemically synthesized from a stoichiometric mixture of LiCl and ZrCl₄ using planetary mill at 500 rpm for 45 h with WC balls in WC pot at ball-to-powder mass ratio of 10:1

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at 25°C using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy on cold-pressed pellets at 380 MPa with Au electrodes
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: the as-milled LZC shows a high ionic conductivity of 8.08×10⁻⁴ S cm⁻¹ at 25°C

electrochemical_window.measurement_conditions: calculated using established scheme based on Materials Project and verified by cyclic voltammetry measurement
electrochemical_window.text_evidence: the electrochemical stability window (ESW) of LZC was calculated to be 1.75 - 4.25 V vs. Li/Li⁺

records.title: A cost-effective and humidity-tolerant chloride solid electrolyte for lithium batteries

properties.synthesis_conditions: annealing the as-milled Li₂ZrCl₆ at 350°C for 5 h with the sample sealed in a vacuum quartz tube

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at 25°C using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy on cold-pressed pellets at 380 MPa with Au electrodes
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: annealing at 350°C for 5 h decreases the ionic conductivity at 25°C by two orders of magnitude, which reaches 5.81×10⁻⁶ S cm⁻¹

records.title: Aliovalent substitution of Al³⁺ in Li₂ZrCl₆ solid electrolyte towards large-scale application

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li₂.₂₅Zr₀.₇₅Al₀.₂₅Cl₆ was synthesized by ball-milling stoichiometric amounts of LiCl, ZrCl₄, and AlCl₃ at 600 rpm for 12 h in WC vial with WC balls using high-energy ball-milling machine

properties.synthesis_conditions: LiCl, ScCl3, and ZrCl4 were ball-milled at 400 rpm for 10 h, then annealed at 300°C for 5 h

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at 25°C using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy with frequency range of 1 Hz to 7 MHz and amplitude of 50 mV on cold-pressed pellets under 400 MPa with Au blocking electrodes
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: The as-milled Li1.9Mn0.9Ga0.1Cl4 shows the highest ionic conductivity of 8.3×10⁻⁵ S·cm⁻¹ among all the Li2-xMn1-xGaxCl4 samples, which is two orders of magnitude higher than the as-milled Li2MnCl4 (7.12×10⁻⁷ S·cm⁻¹)

records.title: Ultrahigh Ionic Conductivity in Halide Electrolytes Enabled by Anion Framework Flexibility Engineering

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li1.25Zr0.25Ta0.75Cl6 was synthesized by mechanochemical method where stoichiometric amounts of LiCl, ZrCl4, and TaCl5 were thoroughly mixed and milled at 500 rpm for 12 h in a zirconia jar with ball/material ratio of 20:1 using a planetary mill under inert argon atmosphere

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at room temperature using AC impedance spectroscopy with frequency range of 7 MHz to 100 mHz and amplitude of 10 mV on cold-pressed pellets under uniaxial pressure of 300 MPa
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: Among the series, Li1.25Zr0.25Ta0.75Cl6 exhibits the highest conductivity of up to 10.3 mS cm−1 at room temperature, ranking one of the highest values ever reported for close-packed chlorides

electrochemical_window.measurement_conditions: determined by linear sweep voltammetry from open circuit voltage to 5 V and 0 V at a scan rate of 0.1 mV/s
electrochemical_window.text_evidence: The anodic stability limit is determined to be 4.23 V for Li1.25Zr0.25Ta0.75Cl6, while the corresponding cathodic limit is 2.50 V

records.title: Lithium-Deficient Halide Solid-State Electrolytes with Enhanced Electrochemical Performance for All-Solid-State Batteries

properties.synthesis_conditions: Stoichiometric LiCl and ZrCl4 were placed in an 80 mL yttrium-stabilized zirconia jar with 5 and 10 mm zirconia balls under Ar, premixed at 200 rpm for 2 h, and then high-energy milled at 600 rpm for 10 h with 30 min milling and 30 min rest cycles; the S-doped samples were prepared analogously using Li2S

ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: ionic conductivity increased from 0.34 to 1.43 mS cm⁻¹ as x rose from 0 to 0.275 at 30°C (Figure 1b).

electrochemical_window.text_evidence: For LZC (Figure 5b), the oxidation decomposition potential is ≈3.90 V (vs Li⁺/Li), while the reduction decomposition potential is 2.68 V (vs Li⁺/Li).

records.title: Lithium-Deficient Halide Solid-State Electrolytes with Enhanced Electrochemical Performance for All-Solid-State Batteries

ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: Remarkably, the LTZC (x = 0.275) achieves a high ionic conductivity of 1.43 mS cm⁻¹ at 30°C, surpassing that of Li-rich Li₂ZrCl₆-based SSEs.

electrochemical_window.text_evidence: In contrast, for LTZC (Figure 5c), the oxidation decomposition potential increases to 4.22 V (vs Li⁺/Li), whereas the reduction decomposition potential remains nearly unchanged at 2.70 V (vs Li⁺/Li).

records.title: Water-Mediated Synthesis of a Superionic Halide Solid Electrolyte

properties.synthesis_conditions: Li₃InCl₆ was synthesized from a H₂O-mediated route where commercial LiCl and InCl₃ precursors with a molar ratio of 3:1 were dissolved in water, after evaporation of the water, the product was heated under vacuum at 200°C for 4h to obtain dehydrated Li₃InCl₆

ionic_conductivity.measurement_conditions: measured at 25°C using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy with the ionic conductivity calculated from the sum of the grain boundary and bulk resistance
ionic_conductivity.text_evidence: The ionic conductivity of Li₃InCl₆ is 2.04×10⁻³ Scm⁻¹ at 25°C, which is among the best reported halide SSEs

electrochemical_window.measurement_conditions: determined by cyclic voltammetry with a Li/Li₃InCl₆/Au cell at 25°C from 0 to 5.0 V at a scan rate of 0.1 mVs⁻¹
electrochemical_window.text_evidence: The cell displayed reversible cathodic and anodic current response around 0 V, and the absence of oxidation currents at high voltages indicated a practical electrochemical stability
